hook.text: A company skips overtime pay for three years. The lawsuit costs $4.2 million. Compliance would have cost nothing.

buildup.text: HR compliance ensures the organization follows labor laws, safety regulations, and anti-discrimination statutes.

discovery.text: Laws vary by country, state, and even city—making compliance a moving target that requires constant attention.

twist.text: Ignorance of the law is never a defense. Companies are liable whether they knew the rules or not.

climax.text: Proactive compliance—audits, training, updated handbooks—prevents problems before they become lawsuits.

punchline.text: Compliance costs pennies. Violations cost millions.

quiz.question: Why is HR compliance challenging?
options: Laws vary by location and change frequently
There are very few labor laws
Compliance only applies to large companies
